title: blurhash::encode result unwrap_or_default in media.rs loses error
description: media::compute_blurhash() calls blurhash::encode(...).unwrap_or_default(). The blurhash crate returns Err when the input dimensions are zero. If width or height is 0 (which can happen from a malformed image decoder result), the error is silently swallowed and an empty string is stored as the blurhash. An empty blurhash in the ActivityPub attachment is spec-invalid. The function should return anyhow::Result<String> and propagate the error to store_processed_image, which can then fail the upload rather than silently store a corrupt attachment.
close_reason: Fixed: blurhash failure now logged, empty blurhash omitted from AP attachment JSON.
